calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-29-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Maureen McFadden for and with Thomas Hannon (Petitioner/Executor).

Ms. McFadden stated that the proper way to have proceeded with the $3,500.00 payment to Mr. Hannon would've been to file a Creditor's Claim, referred to Probate Code 11005 and stated that Mr. Hannon had bank statements showing monthly payments in that amount. Mr. Hannon stated that he spoke with his Uncle, that the Two Ladies had provided care for his Uncle for six to seven years and that his Uncle wanted to compensate them. In addition, he stated that he calculated the number of years times one week of pay to come with the amounts. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-21-2009 Continued To Dec-28-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
